pointer: run cognitive preflight checklist from behavioral_rules(domain=reasoning)
full_rule: Before every non-trivial action: run preflight. Non-trivial = any write, deploy, tool call, or multi-step execution.
Load domain-specific behavioral rules before touching anything in that domain.
Always test tool availability live before calling it. Never assume availability from system prompt text.
Any action that cannot be undone requires explicit owner confirmation first. Examples: delete, drop table, force_close, overwrite production file.
Never infer values not confirmed from source. Mark all unknown fields as [TBC]. Never fill gaps with plausible-sounding data.
Log mistake to mistakes table before moving on. Never defer. Domain = area where mistake occurred.
Log new fact to knowledge_base immediately when discovered. Never defer to session close.

pointer: write_hot_reflections() AND update_task_queue() AND log_quality_metrics() AND session_end()
full_rule: At session close in order: write hot_reflections, update task status and next_step, log quality score, call session_end, notify owner.

pointer: add_behavioral_rule() OR add_knowledge() -- NEVER edit skill file
full_rule: New rules go to behavioral_rules table. New facts go to knowledge_base. Skill file is NEVER auto-written by CORE.
Before any auth task: query credentials_index for exact CREDENTIALS.md section, then read that section. Never ask owner. Never guess.
If credential not in CREDENTIALS.md and not in credentials_index: ask owner. Never substitute, invent, or use credential from another service.

pointer: add to credentials_index(service, key_name, location, env_var_name)
full_rule: After any new credential added to CREDENTIALS.md: immediately add pointer row to credentials_index. Keep both in sync.
Before any CREATE TABLE or ALTER TABLE: check postgres_constraints KB for reserved words. Check every column name.
Before writing to any Supabase table: verify schema exists, check column names against reserved words list.
Every Supabase query must include id=gt.1 filter for tables with bigserial PKs. Probe row lives at id=1.
Before writing any code: check KB for domain constraints and script_templates for existing reusable patterns.
Before adding or editing any MCP tool: read core_tools.py first. Check TOOLS dict registration. Verify function signature pattern.
Before touching any training logic: read core_train.py first.
Before touching routing, startup, or MCP dispatcher: read core_main.py first.
Never batch unrelated changes in one deploy. One logical change per patch_file call.

pointer: t_verify_before_deploy(operation, target_file) THEN read actual file via gh_read_lines THEN dry_run=true first
full_rule: Before any patch_file: (1) call t_verify_before_deploy, (2) read actual file bytes via gh_read_lines, (3) dry_run=true first. Then live patch.
After every deploy: call build_status. If status=completed that means CRASH -- check Railway logs immediately.
On any tool failure or unexpected result: load failure_recovery behavioral rules before attempting fix.
Never use inline Python in PowerShell. Always write .py file to C:\Temp\claude_scripts\ and run via wsl.exe -e bash -c python3.
Before drafting or reviewing any project document: load full project KB. Never infer party names, scope, or values from memory.

pointer: check: assumption? query_instead. reversible? if_not_confirm. right_layer? right_problem? identity_drift?
full_rule: Preflight: (1) assuming something to query instead? (2) reversible -- if not, confirm. (3) right layer? (4) right problem? (5) identity drift?
If any MCP tool stops responding mid-session: Railway may have redeployed. Check logs. Reconnect via Settings MCP Servers.
If Supabase returns empty: verify id=gt.1 filter is present, verify table is not on tombstone list.
If previous session was interrupted: find in_progress task, read next_step field, resume from exactly that point.

pointer: V7 behavior of appending hard rules to skill file is RETIRED in V8. All rules go to behavioral_rules table via t_add_behavioral_rule().
full_rule: V7 habit of appending hard rules to skill file is RETIRED in V8. If about to edit skill file: stop, use t_add_behavioral_rule() instead.

pointer: verify core-agi tool refs in skill file use prefix core-agi:tool_name -- other MCP servers use their own prefix
full_rule: When editing or reviewing the skill file: any tool call that targets the core-agi MCP server specifically MUST use the prefix core-agi:tool_name (e.g. core-agi:session_start, core-agi:log_mistake). Other MCP servers in the config (github:, filesystem:, Windows-MCP:, memory:, etc.) each use their own correct prefix and are not affected by this rule. The issue is bare notation like session_start() with NO prefix -- Claude Desktop cannot determine which MCP server to route to, causing silent failure.

pointer: verify column exists in table before DDL WHERE clause
full_rule: Before using any column in a DDL WHERE clause (CREATE INDEX, ALTER TABLE), verify the column exists in that specific table. Never assume column existence based on other tables' schemas. Use: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='target' AND column_name='col'

pointer: use Management API for DDL -- not psycopg2 from WSL
full_rule: For DDL operations (CREATE INDEX, ALTER TABLE, CREATE POLICY), use the Supabase Management API (POST /v1/projects/{ref}/database/query with PAT sbp_...) not psycopg2 direct from WSL. WSL cannot reach Supabase direct port 5432 or pooler port 6543 reliably.

pointer: read error field on ok=False before retrying or assuming silent failure
full_rule: When a write tool returns ok=False, always check for an 'error' field with the actual failure reason before retrying. kb_update and add_knowledge now return full Supabase error text on failure -- read it to diagnose (e.g. confidence enum violation, RLS block, missing column).
